critic_raw:
Issue 1: Merchant self-selection and possible pre-existing fraud improvement trends.
Issue 2: Authentication confound — stratification by authentication adoption required.
Issue 3: Fraud rate not normalized for different approval volumes.
defender_raw: Round 1: On all three issues — concede. Pre-pilot trend analysis, authentication stratification, and approval-volume normalization are all necessary to establish model causality. The current pilot data is insufficient for the network-wide expansion recommendation.

debate_rounds.critic: Full concession. Critique wins. Network-wide expansion requires properly controlled evidence.
debate_rounds.defender: Round 1: Full concession. A within-merchant randomized test with these controls is the appropriate next step.

points_resolved: Issue 1: merchant self-selection / pre-existing trend — critique_wins
all_issues_raised: Issue 3: approval volume normalization for fraud rates

empirical_test.condition: Difference-in-differences with pre-pilot data; stratify by authentication adoption; normalize fraud rates by approval volume; within-merchant randomized test
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Pre-pilot trends explain outcome differences; authentication adopters drive majority of improvement; normalized rates converge
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: No pre-existing trends; improvement persists in non-authentication-adopting merchants; normalized rates still favor pilot
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Some pre-existing trend but acceleration post-deployment; effect partially attributable to model
